content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The only line of each test case contains a single integer n denoting the number of integers in the array a.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output n space separated integers in a line denoting the content of array a for which the above program will give a wrong answer.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The sum of prefSum[i] and sufSum[i] will exceed the maximum value that can be stored in an unsigned int, causing an overflow.

solution.approach: To generate a counter test case, we need to find an array a such that the sum of prefSum[i] and sufSum[i] exceeds the maximum value that can be stored in an unsigned int (2^32 - 1). We can achieve this by creating an array with large numbers that will cause the sum to overflow.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) — where n is the number of elements in the array
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — where n is the number of elements in the array

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    n = int(input())
    print(' '.join([str(2147483647) for _ in range(n)]))
